runs.content: AMD picked up at the previous close ($503.57) as the clearest catalyst-rich leader of the chip-sector rebound (SOX +3% Jul 20) after last week's semiconductor bear market — confirmed Microsoft/OpenAI/Meta/Oracle AI demand, three analyst price-target hikes this week, and the dated Advancing AI 2026 conference (Jul 22–23) landing while the position is open. Three positions (CAT, IQV, IBM) hit the 60-day max-hold limit on the same day and closed out (+0.05%, +17.18%, +7.2% blended respectively — IQV was the portfolio's first closed win); LRCX was stopped out at −18.16% on the lingering chip selloff.

J.B. Hunt (JBHT) picked up in Portfolio 1 at the previous close ($298.41) — beat Q2 EPS by ~12% and revenue by ~9.5% on ~2.47× average volume, chosen over the deepening chip-sector selloff dominating today's tape. Two exits: XOM hit its 60-day max hold and closed at −7.48%; PENG stopped out at −15.93% on the chip weakness.

Abbott (ABT) entered Portfolio 2 at today's open (+2.20% gap) after raising FY26 adjusted EPS guidance on ~2.47× average volume. Two stop-outs today: ABSI (−25.45%, post-initiation pullback) and PENG (−20.13%, chip-sector selloff) — both full-position exits with no T1/T2 previously triggered.
